name: Snow Report 2026-03-18 16:07:17
report:
Good evening, Pico family,

Get excited for another welcome back to the hill tomorrow! Mostly cloudy skies will stick around, with highs near 35 degrees at the base and 28 at the peak. A steady south wind keeps things feeling sharp, so come prepared for a slightly chilly ride out there. 

Conditions will be on the firmer side with groomed runs improving after another overnight resurface. You’ll find areas of loose granular in addition to machine groomed hardpack tomorrow. Our grooming team is on their usual Thursday plan with our Prospector and Panhandler terrain parks added back into the mix. Fool’s Gold and Forty Niner among others will also get a groom. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 9:00 a.m. to get you where you want to go. 

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

See you out back out there! 

-Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.     

Uphill Travel     

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - CLOSED
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open    

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.                

Mountain Services     

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.    

Tickets & Lessons     

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation     

Pico Fitness Center                                

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.        

Hours of Operation:     

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.            

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

Hey, Pico family,

Monday brought some challenging weather with strong winds making things a little difficult on the hill. We appreciate everyone who came out and made the effort to ski and ride with us despite the conditions.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-16 05:45:45
report:
Good morning, Pico fam!

We’ve been riding the weather roller coaster here—8” of snow over the last 48 hours brought our surfaces back to prime conditions yesterday, while today looks like another blip of false spring.

The weather today brings, let’s say, an “interesting” blend of increased winds, unfavorable precipitation, and eventually warmer temperatures. At the summit early this morning, precipitation may start out as the favorable kind before turning less favorable as the morning goes along. Temps will rise through the afternoon, getting close to 50° at the base and bringing along softer surfaces once again.

It’s also going to be a windy one today, with south winds running 30–40 mph and potential gusts over 50 mph. Safe to say this may not be the best day of skiing we’ve had this year, but we’ll make the most of it.

Conditions were excellent yesterday, as everything from A Slope to Triple Slope had powder to be found. The extra snow should help maintain a nice base on our key connector trails through this brief warm-up, and our groomers will work their magic once this blip passes. We’ll have a private rental at the mountain Tuesday, so an additional round of grooming should help our surfaces stay in good shape after today.

Speaking of grooming, our team tackled what’s been the usual slate recently, minus trails off of Knomes Knoll that won’t be accessible today. Upper K.A. provides an additional groomed route off the summit besides Forty-Niner, while Upper Pike remains natural. Groomed trails will likely be the safest bet this morning depending on what Mother Nature left us overnight—you can see the full list on our Lifts & Trails Report.

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Bonanza Carpet, and Little Pico Triple are slated to open at 9:00 a.m. today. Check that Lifts & Trails Report before heading out in case those strong southerly winds affect lift operations.

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199.

Get your Pico fix in while you can!

-Josh

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-15 05:58:03
report:
Good morning, Pico family!  

False spring is over, and winter has returned in a big way, turning our beloved mountain back into a classic winter scene. About 5 inches of fresh snow brought fun skiing and riding yesterday, and 3 more inches came in overnight to soften our turns again this morning.

Today's weather will bring more clouds and calm winds until the later afternoon, as winds will pick up a bit before around 3:00 p.m. Temperatures will also tick up with the wind, as we'll start at around 20° at the base this morning but will creep up to around 30° by the time lifts close.

While conditions were a bit variable at times yesterday, today should bring a softer, packed powder feel around the mountain after another night of our groomers working their magic. Pike was shredding really nicely yesterday, with a nice groom down the center and firm moguls on the left and right. Upper K.A. should be a fun trek first thing this morning.

Our groomers went after nearly all of our terrain again, with Upper Pike as the exception. Mid Pike and Lower Pike should continue to be great spots to make turns today. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 8:30 a.m. for your bonus powder day needs. Knomes Knoll will follow up soon after and spin starting at 9:00 a.m. 

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Enjoy your Sunday! 

- Cullen

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-14 16:00:43
report:
Good evening, Pico family,  

Winter showed up in a big way today, turning the mountain into a classic winter scene. About 5 inches of fresh snow brought great skiing and riding, with soft turns and powder stashes scattered around the hill. This storm sets us up nicely for Sunday and beyond as our operations team does their thing and surfaces continue to improve. 

A few snow showers may drift through Sunday morning before tapering off in the early afternoon, with increasing clouds overhead and temperatures topping out around 32 at the base and 26 at the peak. Expect breezy conditions as winds shift and pick up through the day. 

While conditions were a bit variable at times, there was still plenty of packed powder around the mountain with soft pockets waiting to be found. Conditions should continue to improve tomorrow as the new snow gets worked in and firmer spots begin to soften thanks to grooming. Pike was shredding really good with a nice groom down the center and firm moguls on the left and right. 

Our groomers are after nearly all of our terrain again with Upper Pike as the exception. Mid Piker and Lower Pike should be great spots to make turns tomorrow. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 8:30 a.m. for your powder day needs. Knomes Knoll will follow up soon after and spin starting at 9:00 a.m. 

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Have a great Sunday! 

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-14 13:20:36
report:
Good morning, Pico family!

Winter is making a comeback as 5" of fresh snow has dropped down on us since last night. Our grooming team worked their magic again to get our snow surfaces on track yesterday, while today should bring some fun, powdery conditions.

It will be a classic winter feel on the slopes, with base temperatures in the high 20s, summit temperatures in the low 20s, and afternoon winds making it feel a bit colder than it will be first thing in the morning. Most of the snow should fall until sometime in the afternoon.

Snow surfaces became friendlier after another night of grooming, and the packed powder that we should see will feel great underfoot. Depending on how much help from Mother Nature we end up getting, you may still find some patches of hardpack by end of day, but conditions will likely stay softer overall at lower elevations. My run of the day yesterday was Ace of Clubs, which had soft, edge-able turns from top to bottom. Mid K.A. and Lower Pike delivered classically good lines in the afternoon as well. 

Our groomers tackled essentially all of our open terrain last night, with Upper K.A. and Upper Pike coming onto the plan to offer a couple more routes down from the summit. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 8:30 a.m. for your powder day needs. Knomes Knoll will follow up soon after and spin starting at 9:00 a.m. 

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Have a lovely weekend! 

- Evan

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-14 07:56:31
report:
Good morning, Pico family!

Winter is looking to make a comeback as 3" of fresh snow has fallen already and we're projected to see 3”-5” of more powder by the end of the day. Our grooming team worked their magic again to get our snow surfaces on track yesterday, while today should bring some fun, powdery conditions.

It will be a classic winter feel on the slopes, with base temperatures in the high 20s, summit temperatures in the low 20s, and afternoon winds making it feel a bit colder than it will be first thing in the morning. Most of the snow should fall until sometime in the afternoon.

Snow surfaces became friendlier after another night of grooming, and the packed powder that we should see will feel great underfoot. Depending on how much help from Mother Nature we end up getting, you may still find some patches of hardpack by end of day, but conditions will likely stay softer overall at lower elevations. My run of the day yesterday was Ace of Clubs, which had soft, edge-able turns from top to bottom. Mid K.A. and Lower Pike delivered classically good lines in the afternoon as well. 

Our groomers tackled essentially all of our open terrain last night, with Upper K.A. and Upper Pike coming onto the plan to offer a couple more routes down from the summit. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 8:30 a.m. for your powder day needs. Knomes Knoll will follow up soon after and spin starting at 9:00 a.m. 

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Have a lovely weekend! 

- Evan

  

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.    

Uphill Travel    

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - CLOSED
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open   

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.               

Mountain Services    

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.   

Tickets & Lessons    

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation    

Pico Fitness Center                               

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.

Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.
